summary: The doc_build_status_check job is a status gatekeeper that intentionally exits with code 1 when an upstream job has failed. The log shows a conditional check where the first job status is 'failure', causing the overall condition to evaluate false and triggering exit 1.
root_cause: An upstream job in the 'Build documentation' workflow has failed. The doc_build_status_check job correctly detects this via the status check condition and propagates the failure by exiting with code 1, as designed.

evidence.reason: The first condition variable is set to 'failure', indicating an upstream job has failed. This makes the first part of the AND condition false.
The status check logic shows it exits 1 when the overall condition is false. With first condition false and second condition true, the AND result is false, triggering the exit 1.
Confirms the job failed with exit code 1 as a result of the status check logic.
This is explicitly a status check job, designed to verify upstream jobs succeeded.

suggested_fix: Examine the actual documentation build job that produced the 'failure' status to identify why it failed. The failure details will be in the logs of the upstream job, not this status check job.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
